channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Grass verges damaged, 17th March
item.description:
Resident at 26 and 66 colindale AVE are deliberately driving over grass verges in front of the property very unsightly
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Collindale Avenue, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working, 17th March
item.description:
One on the street light is not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Smyrk's Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working, 17th March
item.description:
Further to previous report 8856482 this light is not working again. Light was on for 2 evenings and is now off again Please can this be resolved urgently This area of the street has been in darkness for over a month and therefore a safety issue at night

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, Palace Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No dropped kerb, 17th March
item.description:
Resident at 116 Carlton road has concreted over there garden have not applied for a dropped kerb and are using bricks to access the property
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carlton Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bored of keep reporting!!!, 17th March
item.description:
Sick and tired of constantly reporting the same things!!!!! Never worked, constantly marked as fixed. Need new contractors maybe?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 102, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Man hole, 17th March
item.description:
Manhole in centr of road rattling when vehicle drive over it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Street, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Third report!!!, 17th March
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southborough Lane, Southborough, Greater London

item.title: Third report, 17th March

item.title: SECOND REPORT illuminated sign - out, 17th March
item.description:
Reporting a SECOND TIME! 8906722 Marked as fixed when clearly not!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 286, Southborough Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of white goods, 17th March
item.description:
Two fridges have been dumped on Ravensdale Road and someone has smashed them up and now there is glass on the pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ravensdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two packages are wrapped in black cling film, look like old fridge without any label, 17th March
item.description:
A man potent to be a delivery and left two black boxes without knocking door. There’s no label or any detail left on the boxes. He came from Roslin Way, the cctv couldn’t catch his car registration.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 71, Burnt Ash Lane, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pallet, plaster board, light shade left on corner of grass, 17th March
item.description:
Pallet, plaster board, light shade left on corner of grass

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prospect Place, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A number of potholes on the north bound carriageway on the A2 underpass at the Danson interchange, 17th March
item.description:
Potholes. Vehicles swerving to avoid them. Changing lanes to avoid potholes causing near misses with other vehicles. Dangerous for cyclists.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Underpass, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole roundabout, 17th March
item.description:
It has been a pothole for few weeks and a lot of debris in the roundabout, that will cause a damage to cars.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Racist graffiti, 17th March
item.description:
Please remove urgently. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 129, Cotmandene Crescent, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: D &d general maintenance flypostering, 17th March
item.description:
Please remove and paint the box dark green to match the wall behind. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 129, Cotmandene Crescent, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Severe road failure causing structural damage, sleep deprivation & public safety risk – immediate intervention required, 17th March
item.description:
This section of road has deteriorated into a dangerous and unacceptable state, with multiple deep potholes and severe surface failure that have been repeatedly ignored despite ongoing complaints. The situation has now escalated beyond inconvenience into serious public safety risk. Heavy vehicles—particularly buses passing every 8–10 minutes—are striking these defects with such force that the impact is violent, unavoidable, and entirely predictable. This is not routine wear and tear; it is infrastructure neglect. The council has been made aware of pothole-related deterioration across London, yet this specific location has been allowed to worsen to the point where it now poses a real risk to cyclists, motorists, and pedestrians alike, with the strong likelihood of vehicle damage or loss of control. The consequences are now directly affecting residential safety and wellbeing. Each bus impact causes extreme vibrations that feel like an earthquake, shaking the entire property from early morning until late at night. This has led to chronic sleep deprivation, with disturbances occurring as frequently as every 8–10 minutes between approximately 05:30 and midnight. More alarmingly, the repeated shockwaves are now causing visible structural damage inside the home, including cracking floors and kitchen units pulling away from walls. This is no longer a highways maintenance issue—it is a failure of duty of care with tangible impact on residents’ health, safety, and property. Immediate, permanent repair (not temporary patching) is required before further damage or injury occurs. Continued inaction will leave the council fully accountable for any escalation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Radbourne Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Severe road failure causing structural damage, sleep deprivation & public safety risk – immediate intervention required, 17th March
item.description:
This section of road has deteriorated into a dangerous and unacceptable state, with multiple deep potholes and severe surface failure that have been repeatedly ignored despite ongoing complaints. The situation has now escalated beyond inconvenience into serious public safety risk. Heavy vehicles—particularly buses passing every 8–10 minutes—are striking these defects with such force that the impact is violent, unavoidable, and entirely predictable. This is not routine wear and tear; it is infrastructure neglect. The council has been made aware of pothole-related deterioration across London, yet this specific location has been allowed to worsen to the point where it now poses a real risk to cyclists, motorists, and pedestrians alike, with the strong likelihood of vehicle damage or loss of control. The consequences are now directly affecting residential safety and wellbeing. Each bus impact causes extreme vibrations that feel like an earthquake, shaking the entire property from early morning until late at night. This has led to chronic sleep deprivation, with disturbances occurring as frequently as every 8–10 minutes between approximately 05:30 and midnight. More alarmingly, the repeated shockwaves are now causing visible structural damage inside the home, including cracking floors and kitchen units pulling away from walls. This is no longer a highways maintenance issue—it is a failure of duty of care with tangible impact on residents’ health, safety, and property. Immediate, permanent repair (not temporary patching) is required before further damage or injury occurs. Continued inaction will leave the council fully accountable for any escalation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Radbourne Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Canisters dumped on grass

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): London Road, White Oak, Swanley
